name: Facilities Management
student_summary: Run buildings after they're built — corporate campuses to malls.
overview: Run buildings after they're built — corporate campuses to malls. Facilities Management sits in the Property & Assets family of Construction, Architecture & Urban Development. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: You want work that serves your community or country
who_should_avoid: You want a laptop-anywhere lifestyle — this work happens in person
daily_responsibilities: Apply building operations to real problems
Collaborate with your team using vendor management
Keep improving at safety compliance — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
work_environment: Primarily on-site — offices, facilities or the field depending on the role
problem_solving_style: People-centred — listen, mediate, guide and align humans
scope: Demand outlook in India is booming; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
ai_impact: low — AI assists rather than replaces
advantages: Demand is booming: opportunities across India and beyond
A broad Property & Assets foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: Location-bound work; postings and relocation are part of the deal
Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” Facilities Management is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.
portfolio_ideas: A documented case study of vendor management
projects: Beginner: explore building operations with a small self-chosen project
Intermediate: build or run something real using CAFM systems
books: 101 Things I Learned in Architecture School
The Death and Life of Great American Cities
smaller_city_scope: Viable in smaller cities — demand exists well beyond the metros.
rural_scope: Limited rural presence; expect to base yourself in a town or city.
home_state_note: Ask the AI coach about your state — regional demand, cities and relocation advice are generated live from retrieved data, never hardcoded.
transition_paths: Toward Operations Management: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Building Services (MEP): build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Hospitality Operations & Hotel Management: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
faqs: What does Facilities Management pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹4 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹12 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on Any, Physics. Typical degrees: Any degree + FM certification, Hotel management routes. Alternatives exist: diploma + site experience ladder.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is low — AI assists rather than replaces. Broad paths like Facilities Management evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
Facilities Management is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
